name: Efron & Efron, PLLC.
description_short: San Antonio law firm specializing in bankruptcy, family law, real estate, and estate planning with over 60 years of community presence.
description_long:
Efron & Efron PLLC is a law firm based in San Antonio, Texas, with a deep-rooted legacy spanning over six decades. The firm was founded by Jack A. Efron, who began his distinguished legal career in 1957 after earning his law degree from St. Mary's University. Jack served as an Assistant Criminal District Attorney and became board certified in Family and Real Estate Law, earning recognition as a Super Lawyer in Family Law by Texas Monthly Magazine in 2003. The firm is currently carried forward by Barry Efron, continuing the family tradition of legal excellence in the San Antonio community.

Efron & Efron offers comprehensive legal services across multiple practice areas including bankruptcy, family law, real estate law, debt collection defense, civil litigation, probate, estate planning, business formation, and gun trusts. The firm emphasizes a client-centered approach, taking time to understand each client's unique circumstances and providing tailored legal advice. Their expertise spans both litigation and transactional matters, with particular strength in family law and real estate matters based on the founder's board certifications and professional recognitions.

What distinguishes Efron & Efron is its multigenerational legacy and deep integration into the San Antonio legal community. The firm's founder was a past President of the San Antonio Family Lawyer's Association, Chair of the San Antonio Lawyers' Real Estate Discussion Group, and active member of professional organizations including the San Antonio Bar Association and Texas Academy of Family Law Specialists. The firm has maintained community involvement beyond legal practice, with the founder serving as past President of Congregation Agudas Achim and supporting charitable organizations.

While the firm offers bankruptcy services as part of its practice, it is not primarily or exclusively a bankruptcy firm. The website lists bankruptcy among many practice areas but does not provide specific information about bankruptcy filing experience, Chapter 7 vs. Chapter 13 expertise, or dedicated bankruptcy department resources. Clients seeking specialized bankruptcy representation may want to confirm the firm's specific experience and capacity in this area.
pros: 60+ year family legacy with founder Jack A. Efron recognized as Super Lawyer in Family Law by Texas Monthly (2003)
Board-certified expertise in Family Law and Real Estate Law with deep professional network
Multi-practice capability covers bankruptcy, debt collection defense, family law, real estate, probate, and estate planning
Past President of San Antonio Family Lawyer's Association and Chair of Real Estate Discussion Group
Client-centered approach with emphasis on understanding unique circumstances and providing tailored advice
Established San Antonio location near Santikos Northwest with convenient accessibility
Direct contact available with lead attorney Barry Efron (befron@efron-efron.com, 210-366-9676)
cons: Website provides minimal specific information about bankruptcy practice experience, filing volume, or bankruptcy specialization
No details on bankruptcy chapter expertise (Chapter 7 vs. Chapter 13) or success metrics
Bankruptcy listed as one of many practice areas rather than a primary service focus
No online bankruptcy intake process or specific bankruptcy service descriptions available
Limited information about fee structures, payment plans, or bankruptcy-specific pricing
best_for: San Antonio residents seeking general legal services including estate planning, family law, and real estate alongside bankruptcy
Clients with multi-faceted legal needs (e.g., family law matters combined with bankruptcy or estate planning)
Individuals preferring established, locally-rooted law firms with deep community ties and professional reputation
diagnosis: Efron & Efron is best for San Antonio-area clients needing bankruptcy services combined with family law, real estate, or estate planning matters, or those preferring an established firm with deep community roots. The primary caveat is that bankruptcy does not appear to be this firm's core specialty—clients seeking dedicated bankruptcy expertise should confirm the firm's specific experience and current bankruptcy practice capacity before engagement.
services: Family Law (divorce, custody, common law marriage)
